LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL BEESON AN INDIVIDUAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN BY PROVIDING PRIMA FACIE EVIDENCE NO ORAL PARTNERSHIP EXISTED BETWEEN THE PARTIES. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS ("UMF") 1-4, 7, 13.) CONSEQUENTLY, THE BURDEN SHIFTS TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACTS EXISTS REGARDING WHETHER THE CONDUCT OF THE PARTIES AND THE SURROUNDING CIRCUMSTANCES CREATED AN ORAL PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW. (SEE DECLARATION OF CUKIERMAN 9-16; SEE ALSO CAL. CORP. CODE, 16101; HOLMES V. LERNER (1999) 74 CAL.APP.4TH 442.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF KIRSTEN COLEMAN'S MOTION TO QUASH PROTECTIVE ORDER AND TO COMPEL DEFENDANT MICHAEL BEESON'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST MICHAEL BEESON AND HIS ATTORNEY SPENCER MARTINEZ - THE ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT MICHAEL BEESON'S MOTION FOR ISSUANCE OF PROTECTIVE ORDER THAT THE COURT FILED ON DECEMBER 11, 2012, IS NOT "QUASHED." BUT IT IS AN ORDER THAT MERELY ALLOWED MR. BEESON NOT TO COMPLY WITH THE DEMAND IN THE NOTICE OF HIS DEPOSITION TO PRODUCE FINANCIAL DOCUMENTS AND NOT TO ANSWER FINANCIAL QUESTIONS AT HIS DEPOSITION. IT WAS BASED ON THE LACK OF EVIDENCE OF PARTNERSHIP PRESENTED ON THE MOTION RELATED TO MR. BEESON'S DEPOSITION THAT THE COURT HEARD ON DECEMBER 11, 2012. IT IS NOT A GENERAL PROHIBITION ON FINANCIAL DISCOVERY. IT DOES NOT EVEN PROHIBIT REQUESTS FOR THE SAME DOCUMENTS IN A NOTICE OF ANOTHER DEPOSITION OR IN A REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO CCP 2031 RATHER THAN IN A DEPOSITION NOTICE. IN ADDITION, IT DOES NOT APPLY TO TRIAL. WHETHER TO ALLOW FINANCIAL EVIDENCE AT TRIAL WILL BE UP TO THE TRIAL JUDGE. MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. APPEARANCES - ATTY. BENNY MARTIN, TEL. NO. 558-1760 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SPENCER C. MARTINEZ OF THE ROPERS MAJESKI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 972-6305 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF KIRSTEN COLEMAN's motion To Quash Protective Order And To Compel Defendant Michael Beeson'S Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Michael Beeson And His Attorney Spencer Martinez - The Order Granting Defendant Michael Beeson's Motion for Issuance of Protective Order that the Court filed on December 11, 2012, is not "quashed." But it is an order that merely allowed Mr. Beeson not to comply with the demand in the notice of his deposition to produce financial documents and not to answer financial questions at his deposition. It was based on the lack of evidence of partnership presented on the motion related to Mr. Beeson's deposition that the Court heard on December 11, 2012. It is not a general prohibition on financial discovery. It does not even prohibit requests for the same documents in a notice of another deposition or in a request for production of documents pursuant to CCP 2031 rather than in a deposition notice. In addition, it does not apply to trial. Whether to allow financial evidence at trial will be up to the trial judge. Motion to compel is granted and denied in part. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge Pro-tem : Bruce Highman; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/jpt

Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: BENNY MARTIN, ESQ., 415.558.1760, FOR PLAINTIFF KIRSTEN COLEMAN.
SPENCER MARTINEZ, ESQ., 415.513.4800, FOR DEFENDANT MICHAEL BEESON.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT MICHAEL BEESON AN INDIVIDUAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. Defendant shifts the burden by providing prima facie evidence no oral partnership existed between the parties. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Defendants Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 1-4, 7, 13.) Consequently, the burden shifts to Plaintiff. Plaintiff's evidence demonstrates a triable issue of material facts exists regarding whether the conduct of the parties and the surrounding circumstances created an oral partnership agreement as a matter of law. (See Declaration of Cukierman 9-16; see also Cal. Corp. Code, 16101; Holmes v. Lerner (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 442.) Order signed in open court. Off Calendar Sep-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: BENNY MARTIN, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF BENNY MARTIN, 415.558.1760, FOR PLAINTIFF KRISTEN COLEMAN.
SPENCER MARTINEZ, ESQ., ROPERS MAJESKI KOHN BENTLEY, 415.543.4800, FOR DEFENDANT ELECTRO POP ROCKS ET AL.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause To Defendant Michael Beeson is denied. The motion is an improper motion for reconsideration: Plaintiff has failed to explain precisely what the new evidence is, and why the new evidence could not have been provided earlier. On the merits, the motion fails. Plaintiff does not show a probability of prevailing or that the balance of harms tips in her favor. Moreover, it appears that money damages would appropriately remedy any harm. The thrust of the motion is to seek a mandatory injunction (see Reply filed September 12, 2012 at 2 line 16 (management of partnership activities)), as to which the burden on plaintiff is heightened. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
